name: select
title: Select
description: Native <select> with theme tokens, a forced-colors-safe inline-SVG chevron, Field wiring, and 44px minimum target size. Platform keyboard and screen-reader behavior, nothing re-implemented.
useWhen: Choosing exactly one option from a known list of roughly 5-15 items
Space is constrained and radio buttons would not fit
The platform picker is the right UX (mobile wheels, OS listboxes)
avoidWhen: Fewer than ~5 options (use radio buttons so all choices are visible)
Long or unfamiliar lists that need type-ahead filtering (use the Phase 2 combobox)
Toggling a single on/off state (use a checkbox or switch)
Navigating on change (unexpected context change, WCAG 3.2.2)

keyboard: Tab moves focus to the select; Shift+Tab moves away
Down Arrow / Up Arrow move through options (platform may open the listbox first)
Alt+Down Arrow (or Space on some platforms) opens the option list; Enter or Alt+Up Arrow commits; Escape closes without changing
Home / End jump to the first / last option (platform-dependent)
Typing characters jumps to the next option starting with them (native type-ahead)
All behavior is the native platform's own - none of it is re-implemented in script
